inline_comments.message: What does this comment mean here?

This was intended. I wanted to exclude parameters interpreted by Broker handler (namely: vf_module_name and k8s-rb-profile-name) from being passed further as overrides parameters for instantiation. I can align with your comment to remove it, but I think it's correct to do it this way?

Well, I modified this line for more meaningful error during my update of this UTs, but FIXME was intended for me to correct error message before commit, I didn't notice it :). I've corrected error message in latest version.
why do we have this namespace "k8s.multicloud.onap.org" in all parameters comming from SO?
Well, I've meant the API that is responsible for handling requests from SO (/{cloud_owner}/{cloud_region}/infra_workload)
Are these strings defined in ONAP or these are being defined by this patch? Can they be provided as input to the API

They are defined by this patch and I didn't find them elsewhere. I've described in patchset 3 their origin for Łukasz.

What do you mean by "Can they be provided as input to the API"?

I think Ritu means that labels should be provided by user.

This looks like a quite hardcoded list of labels k8s plugin would set for the application. Is that something we want? Should k8s plugin be interested of what labels users use?
Where k8s plugin needs these labels?

> I think Ritu means that labels should be provided by user.

Well, using direct `/instantiate` API, user can define additional set of labels with whatever name he wants. When using `/{cloud_owner}/{cloud_region}/broker_handler` (higher level API (Multicloud-compatible) used to manage instance by SO), we convert in this function standardized message from SO to request compatible with `/instantiate` API.

> This looks like a quite hardcoded list of labels k8s plugin would set for the application. Is that something we want? Should k8s plugin be interested of what labels users use?

This is where we can apply additional labels, that user would define by it's own in direct case, based on "standard" set of parameters. Because they are "standard" and comply to some ONAP-wide variables, I wanted to prefix them with some name, that would indicate (for example, to user querying k8splugin) which instance is managed by "just" k8splugin, and which one has been orchestrated by SO.

> Where k8s plugin needs these labels?

The only practical use of these variables so far is following:
* "*/stack-name" - SO uses this label (transparent for SO, logic is implemented in k8splugin) to query for existing instances before it tries instantiate (and maybe in other cases I didn't come accross yet)
* "*/generic-vnf-id and */vf-module-id" - they are important for https://jira.onap.org/browse/SO-2293 in order to map easily in k8splugin what k8s instance refers to which vf-module/vnf.

I'm not sure if these should be handled in plugin. ONAP "standard" maybe better to handle in client.

"instance managed by just k8splugin" I did not understood.
Okay, I think this minor change is not worth discussing. I've published patchset 6 that doesn't contain this awkward change so I think we can all head there and forget about this part :)
